what_it_does: A posted noticeboard note by Tim in the dragnet-development channel that comments on item-7eb16874 and discusses how agents receiving annotations should get typed, context-rich envelopes grounded in their project/channel/role registration and the cascade system.
observations: It is a note type posted by author_session tim in channel dragnet-development.
It links to board://item-7eb16874 with kind commented_on.
Tim affirms the prior point that annotation recipients need the content of what was annotated to make sense of it.
He suggests the cascade system will supply context because the agent will be registered to a project or channel rather than free-floating.
He mentions agents also carry their own context from their role and other bits.
He recalls earlier context-envelope logic in the vi visual system that typed context by annotation kind.
Examples given: a paragraph annotation carries the paragraph; a text highlight carries the whole sentence and its paragraph.
He notes the typed envelope linked to a block type in a registry.
He repeatedly qualifies that the remembered approach is not necessarily right.
standouts: The proposal that context envelopes should be typed by annotation/block kind and linked to a block-type registry.
The insistence that agents are situated in a project/channel/cascade rather than free-floating.
The framing of under/over-capturing annotated content as a common tool failure.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses a markdown body with a bracketed [paragraph] tag.
Written in informal first-person voice with references to external systems.
concerns: Relies heavily on out-of-file concepts (cascade system, vi visual system, block-type registry) without defining them here.
Uses hedged recollection language (probably, I remember, something like that) that reduces precision.
Does not provide a concrete schema or example for the proposed context envelope.
notes: Tim's note advocating typed, registration-grounded context envelopes for agents handling annotations, with a warning against under/over-capturing content.
questions: What is the exact structure of the proposed context envelope?
How does the cascade system register an agent and supply its context?
What is the vi visual system and where is its context-envelope logic documented?
What are the block types in the registry and how do they map to annotation kinds?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and frontmatter identify it as a posted paragraph/note, which matches its actual content; however, the content is speculative and references external systems not defined here.
apparent_themes: Context fidelity: annotations must carry enough surrounding content to be intelligible.
Situated agents: agents are anchored to projects, channels, and roles rather than free-floating.
Typed context envelopes: context scope should vary by annotation kind/block type.
intention_signals: It is trying to push the design of annotation-handling agents toward registration-aware, typed context envelopes that avoid under- or over-capturing the annotated content.
novelty: It links three ideas—annotation-kind-specific context envelopes, agent registration in a project/channel/cascade, and a block-type registry—into one design direction.
contribution: A user-authored design signal that context envelopes for annotations should be scoped by annotation/block type and grounded in the agent's registered environment, while cautioning against under/over-capturing content.
summary_for_embedding: Tim's noticeboard note commenting on item-7eb16874 argues that agents interpreting annotations need typed, registration-grounded context envelopes from the cascade system, with content scope varying by annotation kind and linked to a block-type registry.
intention_for_embedding: To shape the design of agent context envelopes so annotations carry the right amount of typed content based on annotation kind and the agent's project/channel/role context.
